metadata.title: RAN TCO Analysis — Stay vs Switch
metadata.author: Strategy Team

slides.title: RAN TCO Analysis
slides.subtitle: 5-Year Stay vs Switch Model — 4 Deployment Scenarios

slides.action_title: Switching vendors costs 22-57% more than staying across all scenarios
slides.subtitle: 5-Year TCO comparison, NPV basis
slides.key_message: The switching premium is driven by migration costs and multi-vendor complexity, not hardware pricing differences.

slides.action_title: Migration and multi-vendor costs dominate the switching premium in all scenarios
slides.subtitle: Cost breakdown by category ($M, 5-year NPV)
slides.insight: Migration alone accounts for 35-40% of the total switching cost
slides.source: Internal TCO Model, vendor quotes Q1 2026

slides.action_title: Stay scenario delivers 16 months faster 5G rollout than any switch scenario
slides.subtitle: Timeline comparison: Stay vs Switch deployment

slides.action_title: Three actions to capture value without switching risk

recommendations.title: STAY & NEGOTIATE
recommendations.detail: Leverage RFP pressure for 10-15% better pricing from current vendor. Expected savings: $12-18M over 5 years.

recommendations.title: MODERNIZE IN PLACE
recommendations.detail: Use negotiated savings to fund 5G acceleration. Target: 6-month faster rollout vs current plan.

recommendations.title: STRATEGIC RESERVE
recommendations.detail: Maintain vendor diversification option for future RAN cycles. Keep secondary vendor qualified but not deployed.
